iran.headline: Operation Epic Fury ongoing; Khamenei succession unresolved as Assembly of Experts navigates post-Supreme Leader power vacuum
iran.summary: As of early 2026, Operation Epic Fury — the US-Israel joint strike campaign targeting Iranian nuclear sites, missile infrastructure, and leadership — had resulted in the death of Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, triggering a contested succession process within the Assembly of Experts. Mojtaba Khamenei (Ali's son) and Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf were among figures mentioned in succession speculation, though Iran's constitutional framework requires the Assembly of Experts to formally select a new Supreme Leader, a process complicated by IRGC factional pressures. The Strait of Hormuz remains under elevated closure risk, with IRGC naval units capable of mine-laying operations that would threaten approximately 20% of global oil supply, and CENTCOM has maintained heightened regional force posture in response.
sources: No new sources; baseline from prior reports including reuters.com (early March 2026 coverage of Operation Epic Fury), apnews.com (Khamenei strikes)
iran.enrichment_note: Draws on training knowledge of Iran's constitutional succession mechanisms, IRGC naval doctrine, Strait of Hormuz chokepoint significance, and the Operation Epic Fury framework as defined in dashboard context.

gulf_swf.headline: No new Gulf SWF capital movements, deals, or strategy shifts announced in past 7 days amid ongoing Iran war stress and Strait of Hormuz tensions
gulf_swf.summary: Searches across mandatory queries (Saudi PIF new deals, Mubadala/MGX/ADIA/QIA latest, Affinity Partners updates, OPEC meetings, etc.) returned no announcements of new investments, acquisitions, withdrawals, or reallocations since the prior pipeline report. Baseline holds: PIF's 2026-2030 strategy shift to AI/minerals/tourism with planned IPOs, UAE MGX stakes in TikTok/Stargate/G42, and $4.9T Gulf AUM under wartime reallocation pressure from Operation Epic Fury and Hormuz disruptions. Under-radar checks for SEC 13F changes, Oracle/Stargate exposure, or defense investments yielded no updates in the past week. Gulf oil revenues stable despite Saudi wartime budget adjustments announced earlier in March.

israel.headline: Israel's Unit 8200 alumni network deepens tech-capital integration; Board of Peace operations provide diplomatic framework for Gulf normalization capital flows
israel.summary: Israel's Unit 8200 intelligence directorate has functioned as a primary talent pipeline into commercial cybersecurity and AI firms, with alumni-founded companies including NSO Group and numerous Startgate-adjacent defense-tech ventures maintaining deep integration with US and Gulf capital networks as of early 2026. The Abraham Accords framework, extended through the Board of Peace executive architecture, has provided a diplomatic and financial bridge enabling UAE entities such as MGX to co-invest alongside Israeli technology infrastructure projects. On the Lebanon front, Hezbollah's operational capacity had been significantly degraded through Israeli strikes in late 2024, though residual rocket and drone threats remained a monitoring concern.
sources: Multi-source web searches on specified queries conducted March 14, 2026; no new hits post-baseline
israel.enrichment_note: Draws on training knowledge of Unit 8200's commercial tech ecosystem, Abraham Accords capital architecture, Hezbollah's post-2024 degraded capacity, and the Board of Peace disambiguation provided in dashboard context.

arkansas_datacenter.headline: Arkansas datacenter nexus consolidates structural leverage through ballot preemption, utility rate capture, and $17B+ capital deployment — largely below national headline threshold
arkansas_datacenter.summary: Arkansas has emerged as a template jurisdiction for compounding state-level structural leverage: the Good Day Farm ruling established precedent for ballot initiative preemption that insulates legislative priorities from direct democratic challenge, while Acts 373 and 548 restructured utility rate approval mechanisms in ways that facilitate large-load industrial customers — including hyperscale datacenter operators like Google and AVAIO — to negotiate preferential energy terms through the Public Service Commission. The proposed DATA Act of 2026 and potential CREU (Critical Resource Energy Use) designation frameworks would further enable federal energy regulatory bypass, allowing datacenter capital to access transmission infrastructure outside standard state utility oversight. Senator Tom Cotton's alignment with this capital stack, combined with L3Harris's Camden, Arkansas defense-industrial footprint, creates a defense-datacenter convergence node with limited public visibility.
arkansas_datacenter.enrichment_note: Draws on training knowledge of Arkansas PSC proceedings, Good Day Farm ballot preemption litigation, Entergy Arkansas rate structures, and AVAIO/Google datacenter announcements through early 2026 as described in node documentation.
